hints.Indicative: Indicative perfect 1st sg. is also 'veni', but here it is an imperative command
hints.Subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'venias' — 'veni' is the imperative singular of 'venire'
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'venire' — 'veni' is the imperative singular

hints.vinco, vincere, vici, victum: These are the principal parts of 'vincere' (to conquer)
hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the principal parts of 'videre' (to see)
hints.vendo, vendere, vendidi, venditum: These are the principal parts of 'vendere' (to sell)

hints.Nominative: While the form is the same, 'Spiritus' is addressed directly as part of 'Creator Spiritus'
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'spiritus' (4th decl.) would also be 'spiritus' with long -ūs, but here it is vocative
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'spiritus' would be 'spiritum' — 'Spiritus' here is vocative

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'spiritus' has genitive in -ūs
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'spiritus' has genitive in -ūs
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns have genitive in -ei — 'spiritus' has genitive in -ūs

hints.Nominative: While 'mentes' could be nominative plural, here it is the direct object of 'visita'
hints.Genitive: Genitive singular of 'mens' would be 'mentis' — 'mentes' is nom./acc. plural
hints.Ablative: Ablative plural of 'mens' would be 'mentibus' — 'mentes' is accusative plural

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'mentis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'mentis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'mentis' ends in -is

hints.Indicative: Indicative present 3rd sg. would be 'visitat' — 'visita' is the imperative singular
hints.Subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'visites' — 'visita' is the imperative singular of a 1st conjugation verb
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'visitare' — 'visita' is the imperative singular

hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the principal parts of 'videre' (to see)
hints.specto, spectare, spectavi, spectatum: These are the principal parts of 'spectare' (to watch/look at)
hints.viso, visere, visi, visum: These are the principal parts of 'visere' (to go to see)

translation: Come, Creator Spirit, visit the minds of your own
